mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Deceleration in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which suppresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative market returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of periods when INDPRO growth turns negative, invalidating the supply-side drag mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, liquidity conditions become exceptionally accommodative, prompting portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS drops below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer confidence, driving positive equity performance.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal renewed construction activity and broader economic confidence, which correlates with higher corporate investment and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or housing starts show zero predictive power for equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 indicates severe supply-side contraction and factory idling, which forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative market returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 65 percent of instances when INDPRO drops below 95.0, refuting the supply contraction thesis.
